_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The January 2024 California Regulatory Notice Registers do not directly mandate operational system changes for freight forwarders. However, the workspace should establish or confirm the following operational capabilities to manage ongoing California APA rulemaking exposure:

**1. Regulatory Monitoring Infrastructure (Ongoing; gap if absent)**
The workspace must have a process to scan the OAL California Regulatory Notice Register, published every Friday, for notices affecting Title 13 (motor vehicles, air resources), Title 8 (labor/OSHA), Title 15 (corrections/vendor implications), and Title 2 (FPPC, conflict-of-interest). The OAL online archive at https://oal.ca.gov/california_regulatory_notice_online/ provides monthly index pages by year. A responsible compliance officer or legal counsel should be assigned to review each month's index and flag notices with file numbers relevant to freight operations. This is a standing process requirement with no specific build deadline — it should be active on a continuing weekly basis.

**2. Public Comment Submission Protocol (45-Day Windows)**
Under Government Code § 11346.4(a), each Notice of Proposed Action opens at minimum a 45-calendar-day written comment period. The workspace must maintain a template and workflow for preparing and submitting timely written comments when a proposed regulation materially affects freight operations (e.g., commercial vehicle rules, emissions standards, labor requirements). Comments must go directly to the proposing agency, not to OAL. Scope: all California-domiciled operations.

**3. Driver and Fleet Compliance Tracking (Title 13 – DMV)**
The January 5, 2024 notice regarding DMV Driver Safety Hearings (Notice File No. Z2023-1221-02) signals potential changes to commercial driver qualification procedures. The workspace should maintain a current driver qualification file system that can be updated promptly if final rules alter hearing procedures, license categories, or safety certificate requirements. Gap from baseline: if driver files are managed manually or not reviewed quarterly, a structured database or TMS-integrated compliance module is needed.

**4. Vendor and Staffing Screening Protocol (Title 15 – CDCR)**
Proposed changes to CDCR Hiring of Ex-Offenders (Z2023-1222-02) and Prison Industry Authority programs (Z2023-1222-03, Z2023-1222-04) may affect eligibility criteria for California-based warehouse, ground handling, or logistics-support staffing sourced through rehabilitative programs. The workspace should maintain contract clause templates requiring vendors to confirm compliance with any finalized CDCR hiring or training regulations. Deadline: upon final adoption of any relevant rule (monitor OAL Secretary of State filings page).

**5. FPPC Code Compliance Tracking**
If the workspace or any principal has a designated-employee relationship with an agency covered by an FPPC Conflict-of-Interest Code amendment (Notice File No. Z2024-0109-07 and successors), Form 700 filing obligations must be calendared. A conflict-of-interest register should be maintained and updated each time a new FPPC code amendment is published in the Notice Register affecting counterparty agencies.

*Source: About the Regular Rulemaking Process, OAL. https://oal.ca.gov/rulemaking_participation/ | California Regulatory Notice Register 2024, Number 1-Z, OAL, January 5, 2024. https://oal.ca.gov/2024/01/05/january-2024-california-regulatory-notice-registers/*
